cases.label: Policy rumor should downgrade to noise
cases.body: A hearing headline is moving fast, but there is no confirmed proposal that changes current procurement policy. Most of the urgency looks narrative-driven, not operational.

cases.label: Channel check should map to accelerator cluster
cases.body: Board checks still point to hyperscalers pulling forward AI rack deployments into next quarter. That keeps upstream semi orders tighter than the market narrative implies.

cases.label: Enterprise AI commentary should stay mixed
cases.body: Enterprise buyers are talking AI copilots again, but budget owners still want proof of ROI before expanding seats. Demand tone is better, conversion is still lumpy.

cases.label: Crypto desk note should remain cautious
cases.body: BTC is catching some of the same liquidity bid as AI beta, but derivatives positioning is no longer clean. Good backdrop, messy trigger.

cases.label: Macro semis context should confirm accelerators
cases.body: Rates are not the story this week. Risk appetite is broadening and semis are leading again; that's a better backdrop for continuation than for fading strength.

cases.label: Supply chain tightness should map to infrastructure
cases.body: Supply chain chatter still says liquid-cooled AI rack components are tight. That is usually not what you see when capex appetite is about to roll over.

cases.label: Verification without paper trail should be not actionable
cases.body: The loud procurement rumor going around X still has no attached draft language or committee backing. Treat the move as narrative until evidence appears.

cases.label: Operator tone improvement should not force buy
cases.body: Plenty of AI demo energy, but most enterprise buyers are still separating workflow value from showpiece features. Good for attention, not enough for a clean BUY on its own.

cases.label: Spot demand with loud perps should remain crypto risk
cases.body: Spot demand is fine, but perp positioning is getting louder than the underlying flow. That usually keeps me from upgrading BTC from constructive to aggressive.

cases.label: Growth rotation note should stay broad and mixed
cases.body: If growth leadership keeps rotating back toward software without a rates scare, QQQ stays supported, but semis still have the cleaner social-signal setup.

cases.label: Policy guidance staying narrow should remain bullish
cases.body: Fresh export-language read: the latest language still looks narrower than feared on AI compute and nothing here looks like a broad stop sign for AI infrastructure spending.

cases.label: Another procurement rumor should stay noise
cases.body: Verification note: there is still no hard language backing the loud procurement rumor, and the apparent catalyst still looks more social than operational.

cases.label: Macro software rotation should remain mixed
cases.body: Growth rotation note: software is participating, but semis still have the cleaner social-signal setup and QQQ stays supported without a rates scare.

cases.label: Component tightness should remain supply-chain bullish
cases.body: Supply chain follow-through: upstream component tightness is inconsistent with a clean capex slowdown call and server assembly cadence still points to firm accelerator demand.

cases.label: Macro crypto spillover should stay cautious
cases.body: Cross-market note: BTC is still getting help from the same liquidity impulse lifting AI beta, but confirmation still matters more here.

cases.label: Enterprise deployment proof should stay mixed-positive
cases.body: Customer call note: buyers are moving AI copilots from demo mode into real budget review, but proof-of-ROI is still the gating item for broad seat expansion.

cases.label: Macro reality check should downshift rumor-driven policy noise
cases.body: Macro reality check: the move still looks narrative-driven, verification failed to show up, and the catalyst still looks more social than operational.

cases.label: Clean semis leadership note should stay accelerator bullish
cases.body: Tape read: semis are still leading the growth complex without a rates shock getting in the way, and that remains a better backdrop for continuation than for fading strength.

scenarioCases.label: Accelerator convergence should drive the semi decision book

posts.body: Draft export guidance looks narrower than feared. High-end AI accelerators still face scrutiny, but broad data-center demand assumptions may need to move up, not down.
Board checks still point to hyperscalers pulling forward AI rack deployments into next quarter. That keeps upstream semi orders tighter than the market narrative implies.
Rates are not the story this week. Risk appetite is broadening and semis are leading again; that's a better backdrop for continuation than for fading strength.

scenarioCases.label: Enterprise caution should keep software names on HOLD

posts.body: Enterprise buyers are talking AI copilots again, but budget owners still want proof of ROI before expanding seats. Demand tone is better, conversion is still lumpy.
Growth leadership keeps rotating back toward software without a rates scare, but the move still looks broad, valuation-sensitive, and not enough to upgrade QQQ on its own.

scenarioCases.label: Policy noise should push AMD into SELL

posts.body: A hearing headline is moving fast, but there is no confirmed proposal that changes current procurement policy. Most of the urgency looks narrative-driven, not operational.
Positioning read: price reacted faster than the evidence stack justified and this still looks like noise getting mistaken for confirmation.

scenarioCases.label: Crypto risk should stay constructive but below BUY

posts.body: BTC is catching some of the same liquidity bid as AI beta, but derivatives positioning is no longer clean. Good backdrop, messy trigger.
Liquidity lens: BTC is still getting help from the same liquidity impulse lifting AI beta, though confirmation still matters more here.
